sentences.text: Hello, hello!
Oh, no, wait.
Hi, everyone.
Okay, amazing.
Hello, how are y'all?
Hey there!
Hello!
I'm so sorry I'm not on camera, I'm actually moving today, and I look like I'm moving today, if you know what I mean.
I… I look like a mess.
How are y'all?
Doing good, how are you?
Hi!
Good, yes, doing good.
I really can't complain.
I feel like things are kind of cooling down here, so that's nice, but how are y'all up there?
Travis, how are your travels?
Oh, it was pretty minor.
I just went over to Bozeman, Montana for a couple days, for our, grand opening celebration we did for one of our properties, which was good.

It was nice, you know, good turnouts, had the mayor up there, and he gave us you know, gave a speech, which was really good, and really just trying to get that property some publicity to get more prospects.
Okay, well, that sounds like a heck of a lot of publicity.
That's really impressive.
The mayor was there?
I mean, it is Bozeman, so let's not, like….
Not the big… Still impressive.
I think it is, yeah, for sure!
And also, I do love Montana so much.
Did you get to, like.
Enjoy any of your travels while you were there?
I mean, I flew in Wednesday afternoon, I left Friday, kind of midday.
The event was… Thursday morning.
So Thursday afternoon, I kind of, you know, walked around the downtown area, and, you know, it's a cute town, a lot of fun shops, you know, it's obviously a college town, too, so there's some fun.
Yeah.
As far as the restaurant, so… Right.
Yeah, usually….
Good!
Sana, how are you?
Anything new in your world?
Oh, I'm good.

I just wanted to say that, usually when we have one of these grand openings, there's some massive storm or something like that, so I've been teasing Travis that he's the bringer of… of storms, that he's gonna just make it dump rain on Bozeman, but… You know, it was kind of stormy the day before, but we made it out fine.
Well, it's complicated, I'm cursed.
So, we did… I joined, you know, last spring, and I kind of… thrown into the, you know, events, because marketing does everything that's fun, right?
I don't know, but it's our.
Right.
And so….
Yes.
We planned the holiday party in December last year, and….
Oh, yeah.
Oh, it would… But, like, it was a torrential downpour, and traffic was awful.
I was like, okay, that's kind of to be expected, but it sucks nonetheless.
Then we had our grand opening for two properties in Renton, in this outside of Seattle area, in June.
for that reason, because it's like, you know, right after school ended, it's going to be summer here, and it literally drizzled the entire day.
Hadn't rained for weeks.
That was the crazy part.
Oh, oh my gosh!
We had our employer appreciation party 2 weeks ago, or 3 weeks ago now, or, like, middle of August.
Again, Seattle, it doesn't rain for, like, 3 months, then it rains the rest of the year.
It rained that day, too.
And I'm just like, I don't… I don't know why.
I mean, no!
I can't… I can't tell if this is a superpower or a curse.
I don't know.
I meet both.
When I landed in Boston on Wednesday, there was some storm that came in, and it started to rain while I was, like, starting to collect the materials.
I'm like, are you fucking kidding me?
Like, this can't be a thing.
Luckily, that was just a short little rainstorm that ended a couple hours later, and, like….
Check it.
It was fine, but I was like, come on, I can't go four with this.
Halloween, that's so funny, and I'm so glad to hear that the rain did chill out.
It's also, I mean, okay, this is where we can talk about it being a superpower, because this is, like, Montana's wildfire season, right?
Yeah, that's… that's true.
Okay.
You're actually saving lives, there you go.
So… so there you go, Travis.
You are a hero.
I do what I can.
Post Spider-Man, with great power comes great responsibility.
Of course.
Yes, there, yes, absolutely.
Okay, well, first of all, I can tell that I might be lagging a little bit.
Can you guys hear me?
I'm at a coffee shop, so… I just want to make sure that I'm not, like, there's not too much background noise or anything.
Oh, you sound good.
Okay, okay, perfect!
Alright!
Well then, yes, I just wanted to check in.

I did think that the trial was still running, but I believe that the trial features on Facebook and Instagram, looks like they turned off at the 14-day mark, and then it looks like Google is, like, connected, but not.
Did you guys disconnect Google on your end, or was…
That's….
think?
I thought that we did.
Yeah, I think they… it should still be running, … Unless you guys have changed something on your end.

Yeah, I think what happened, because the guy that, the guy who runs our trials, he has been out of the office this past week, so I think what happened is, it looks like the capabilities may be… ended at the 14-day month, so I apologize for misspeaking there.

But I did want to show y'all… we already have some new updates in the dashboard, so I wanted to kind of show y'all those before we kind of do a deep dive and You know, kind of get a better understanding of how the trial was, Sona, I know you and I chatted about it a little bit.

… But, first and foremost, an update here is, anytime that you… are in this dashboard, and maybe, like, for instance, we can pretend like we don't like this post, you can click this button right here, and it'll completely regenerate the post altogether.
So, that's a new update that I wanted to highlight.
I additionally, and so on, I know that we'll… we'll chat through.

I know that, kind of the… I'm trying to remember what the wording was that you used, but basically, like, some sort of, system in place to ensure that the on-site teams aren't posting before you can take a peek at that content, so… This is unfortunately not going to address that entirely, but just something that we have put in place as far as permissions goes.
Is, in the event that, yeah, y'all do come on board, your on-site teams can be restricted to either just one property or multiple properties.
So I also wanted to mention that.
Any questions on either of those updates?
So for the regeneration, element, are you able to delete now?

So I know that was one of the limitations, … It might have been because we were doing the scheduled, or doing the, the trial, but when I generated, like, test content, it wouldn't allow me to then delete it.
So, say there were two posts for that day, and I had scheduled in a third, I wouldn't be able to delete that third one.
Yeah, so, and that's something that I should have mentioned on the front end.
So, once… once the content is published, on Google, Facebook, or Instagram, you then would need to go directly to the profile to, delete that content at this point.
scheduled side.
Oh, on the scheduled side.
Oh, so you're saying that the post, you scheduled the post, and then you can delete it from there?
I can't delete it.
So basically, like, let's say.
Oh, okay.
I scheduled, like, these example posts out for, like, you know, two weeks in advance, just to test it, and then I wasn't able to remove that additional post… Got it.
…after I put in the tests.
Yeah, you definitely should have been able to do that, so I am curious if that was a test thing, because certainly that would not be the case moving forward.
So I do apologize for that, and I'm sorry that I misunderstood the… the issue.
Yeah, no worries, that sounds good, as long as that is a function that we would have in the future.
Oh, absolutely.
No, you sh… yeah, that's very strange.
I wonder… I wonder if it was something about, like, scheduling it two weeks out, maybe outside of the trial period?
But….

I did it, like, I made sure it was within the trial period, so this would have been, like, the very first day, so I think I scheduled it out for, like, maybe, like, 11 days or something like that, so it's still within that period.
Okay.
Right, yeah.
I certainly have not heard… any issues with that.
I will definitely confirm, but no, you definitely should have been able to delete that within the dashboard, so that is a little strange.
I don't know if that was just a trial situation or not, so I will confirm with that for sure.
Okay, so deleting a social post, or sorry, a… Manually scheduled social post.
Okay, on that note with the social posts, I wanted to also speak to the automated content.
So we are beginning to roll out localized content, which is really exciting, especially, you know, for Facebook and Instagram, where your residents live.
We'll be able to highlight, like, for instance, the highest rated restaurants in the area, so that's something that is, on the roadmap, being beta tested currently, so more localized content.
As far as the automated posts are coming soon.
And then on the manual side of things, an update is, we are about to roll out the ability to host videos from the dashboard.
So, posting reels on Facebook and Instagram manually where your team, sees fit.
So, wanted to mention that as well.
Any questions on any of that?
No, that makes a lot of sense.
I was gonna say that actually is one of the items that we have in Sochi that you guys don't have, which is the reels and stories aspect.
So, would this cover both Reels and stories, or is this just reels for now?
So this will be just reels for now, and I can get an ETA on, … stories in the roadmap.
I would think that that would be something that we would roll out down the road, but yeah, this time we do not have that capability just yet.
Gotcha.
Yes, okay, let's see… sorry, I'm going through my notes on my phone.

… Alright, so Travis, I know I spoke with Sana a little bit, and I'm sure you guys have debriefed as well, but I guess overall, what were your thoughts on the tools and the trial overall, and kind of how are y'all feeling about potentially rolling this out, down the road?
Honestly, I wasn't that involved with the demo of the Sonic.

Well, you know, I kind of wanted to stay a little hands-off, intentionally, because, you know, I'm not going to be managing this going… you know, this is kind of her thing, so I really wanted to impact anybody, and I know there was… you know, some pros, some cons, comparing the two, as, you know, comparing any software, there is.
But, yeah, I don't… I don't really have a great answer for you, so I'm just going to kind of defer to Sana on, you know, what some of those items.
Okay, okay, perfect.
Cool.
I can just, jump into, like, my… my list really quick.
So, I think, kind of, off the top of my head, like, I do really like that idea of the localized content.
I think that when you guys roll that out, it'll be really helpful.
Your dashboard is beautiful.
A lot of the feedback we get from Sochi is that it's difficult to find things, and I think that you guys have really nailed that, experience.
It's a lot cleaner.
Awesome.
I think the social copy that you guys provided, too, was much better than what Sochi provides.
I think it was just, you know, maybe because it's specifically two apartments, but you guys really had clean, nice copy, and I liked everything that you guys produced.
Right.
And then, obviously, we didn't do the reviews, but when I looked at the review responses, I also felt like those were a step up from what Sochi is doing.
And also your ability to post specials and floor plans directly to the platform was amazing.
Loved of that.
Okay.
And then your products tab as well.
I think Travis can speak a little bit more to, like, what… how that compares to, weblisters, but we don't have all of our properties on weblisters, is my understanding, so I liked the products tab.
I don't know how easy it was to check to make sure everything was looking good there.
Hey!
Okay, great!
Alright, perfect.
And then, as far as the… as far as, like, the system in place.
Because I know with Sochi, I guess two things on Sochi.
Please remind me, with Sochi… oh, actually, no.
So, with the reputation piece, I know… Yes, with those preloaded, automated, responses, I'm so happy that you're pleased with them.
Please know that those can also be changed.
And then, yes, a big piece there for us, you know, and how we differ from Sochi, is the ability to integrate with, Yardi to pull over those, those contacts.
Do y'all use… y'all do use Yardi, right?
Did I just have a… did I just… go blank for a second.
Okay, amazing.
I'm so sorry, you guys, I…
I don't know why moving just, like, absolutely wrecks my brain a little bit.
All that sorting and organizing everything when you're packing up.
Oh, it's so bad, and every time I move, I'm like, am I, like, becoming a hoarder?
Like, how do I have so much stuff?
Anyway, I digress.
Okay, so, yes, and then… There was one thing that I was gonna say, but okay, so with that being said….
Yeah, I have a few more, kind of, cons, I guess, to add to this list, just so that we….
Okay, yes.
some feedback in general.
Absolutely.

Adding new content, yeah, I understand that there were some limitations because we were still on the trial, but it was a little frustrating, and I'm thinking about what our user experience would be like for our site teams.
There's no built-in library, and there's no ability to select pre-made social media messages.
So, so she provides a full library for our, site staff to just, you know, quickly grab their photos.
They don't have to go find another folder and pick anything out.
It just, you know, all… everything lives within the platform.
And then if they have a previous message that they've already written that they just want to reshare, they can do that, like, with a few simple clicks.
Okay.

And then, we already talked about the stories and reels, but that is an element that, like, more of our properties are starting to use, and become invested in, but it sounds like you guys are rolling that out in the next, hopefully, few weeks.
So that's all, important to note.
Okay.
For the Q&As, I like the seating idea.
We have heard some rumors that Google Q&As in general are going away.
Yeah.
Yeah, so is that something you guys have heard as well?

So, to be completely transparent with y'all, actually, this is not the first, time that Google has changed something, so… we used to actually have a chatbot on Google, and then Google did… Google Sunset chatbot capabilities, and then, we rolled out the Q&A in its place.
So, we're kind of, like, always, you know, Google does keep us on our toes.
But we are kind of ever-evolving to make sure that we you know, are in line with Google's changes.
So, with that being said.
I haven't heard anything.

I'm curious, as far as, like, the rumors that y'all have heard about Q&A going away, is this just kind of, like, rumblings, you know, in the multifamily world, or is there something that y'all have heard from Google directly?
You know, I've seen some posts on… in general, in the, like, community on Reddit,
Yeah.
heard this from our SOSHI rep.
She brought it up when we were discussing a few, updates that they're making on their end as well.
Okay, okay, got it.
Let me see if there's anything that we are thinking currently to get ahead of that Q&A piece.
… But yes, I guess, like, is there anything in particular that y'all would like to see in the Q&A's place?
Is that kind of the biggest concern, is just with Q&A going away, as far as the seating piece goes?

Yeah, I think that, you know, the main functionality that you guys offer there is the seating, and if we don't have that element, I don't know that it's adding a whole lot of capability that we don't already have in Sochi.
At least right now, like, one good example is you can go in in Sochi, and while it's not repeatedly posting the answers, you have a lot of control over what those answers look like.
Okay.
this might have been a limitation because of our trial, but I couldn't edit any of our answers, and every answer looked roughly the same, right?
So, like, a good example would be, you know, are you guys pet-friendly?
The auto-answer from you guys is, yes, we are pet-friendly, and that's the same as it is for every other, like, do you have two-bedroom floor plans?
Yes, we have two-bedroom floor plans.
So it's, like, the same format of answer, and it's pretty… I think it kind of becomes obvious over time that it's a bot.
Okay.

in Sochi, it gives us a little more flexibility, so we can go in there and say, like, yes, we are pet-friendly, and we have a dog park with pet waste stations, so you can get a little more specific there.
And I think that's something that would be… Beneficial for you guys.
Okay.
Yeah.
And then, last but not least, the most important piece for us is definitely moderation tools, and I think that's a deal-breaker.
Yeah, that's….
Okay, okay.
Yeah.

we want to be posting about resident events, we want our employees to be contributing to the content, and right now, unfortunately, like, the quality of the work that I'm seeing on Sochi is really poor, like.
You know, people generating things on… ChatGPT, and then leaving the prompt in there, and then trying to post that.

So without the ability for me to go in there and select approve, or, you know, please write this again, or even just editing it myself and getting that notification so it doesn't automatically post poor quality content is essential for us.
Okay, okay, got it.
Alright, no, I appreciate all of that feedback, and I totally understand, you know, that moderation piece being important to you.
Because yes, at this time, I mean… Oh, sorry, what?
Is that gonna be the pipeline for you for, as you kind of continue to….
Yeah, so I just spoke with our product specialist, because it does sound like that is obviously, you know, coming up a lot in conversations.
And it looks like it is something that we are looking into, but it is not something that we have officially rolled out yet.

But… you know, I… I spoke with someone else, my counterpart, Travis, you'd actually met with her in the past, Elise, and I asked if it was something that, you know, she had been asked from, you know, prospects.
Is this something that's interest, of interest to them, this moderation piece, and it does sound like it's really important.
So, with the demand being there, I find it hard that we… to, like, imagine that we won't address that, but at this time, I cannot give you all a solid answer, so I, you know, I do understand.
Sure.
You know, if… remind me when your Sochi contract is up.
I think it's….
I think it's October, early November, sometime around that.
Okay.
When I look at this piece, it seems like it's a more comp….

I don't know, I'm not a coder, but it just seems more advanced of an add-on than just a simple make a couple small tweaks to how the program works, because you're kind of rebuilding rules and, you know, kind of that moderation piece, so… I don't know, like, you know, rolling out that quickly, and then, you know, obviously troubleshooting for any bugs and glitches that'll of course come up, so that's just where we're kind of looking at this of when it comes to timing.

Yeah, so, you know, with our ability to schedule out content already, in my opinion, that already… does kind of, you know, we're… it feels like we're halfway there to some level of moderation piece, because… You know, if you're already able to schedule out that content, that would then give y'all You know, a few weeks or months to go in there and improve that content.

So I would like to think that building it out wouldn't be too difficult, but I also understand your concern, and like, okay, well, if y'all were to roll this out right now, and y'all have to, you know, renew with Sochi in October, November, like, I do understand your concern, and potentially having to… you know, God forbid, troubleshoot that at all.
So, … Okay, well, this is all really, really great feedback, and I really do, appreciate it.
Sana, do you have anything else for me?
I know that… Of course, the moderation.
Sure, yeah, those are the biggest, you know, pieces of this.

Like I said, I really love the content you guys were providing, so if we could get, you know, moderation tools, and then kind of get caught up on stories and reels, we'd be very interested in your platform.
But I think at this time, without the moderation of tools especially.
I… we can't make that jump.
Yeah, okay, no, I… I really and completely understand that.
… Okay, cool.

Well, with that being said, I will definitely, especially since I was just talking to our product specialist about this, I will… you know, how this is important to y'all, and it sounds like it's gonna be really important to a lot of our partners.
So I will let y'all know what I learned there.
And you know, I will keep you posted on any updates that we have with the roadmap.

address your concerns, and, you know, I'd love to partner with you guys, but I also understand if the timing is not right, with where we are on this dashboard, we can ideally always revisit down the road.
Yeah, I think that sounds great.
Yeah, obviously, you know, there's a lot we like, but there's obviously some big pieces still missing, so yeah, keep us in the loop, and….
Yeah, I really hope that this is great.
They can be all in their name.
Yeah, sorry, I think there's some background noise we're hearing.
Well, okay, with that being said, I will not keep y'all on too much longer.
So what I'll do is I'll try to get an idea of, you know, where we are in these things.

you know, what it would look like to roll out some sort of moderation tool, and then I will, go ahead and just follow up with y'all via email, but I really, really appreciate y'all's time and, you know, poking around in the dashboard, Sona, I appreciate you.
And yeah, just trialing the tool overall.
I really appreciate y'all giving us a go.
Yeah, of course, Shelby, it's been great chatting with you and testing this product out, and yeah, we'll see what the future brings us.
Okay, that sounds good.
All right, well, I will be in touch very shortly.
Thank y'all so much for, dealing with me as I'm moving, and their blenders, etc.
And happy Friday, Junior!
I hope you all have a lovely rest of your week!
I hope you survived.
Yeah, good luck.
Thank y'all so much.
Okay, I'll talk to y'all soon.
Can I get back.
Bye-bye.
Goodbye.
